mission.title: Install and Configure RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
mission.description: The RabbitMQ Cluster Operator is a Kubernetes operator designed to deploy and manage RabbitMQ clusters. It simplifies the lifecycle management of RabbitMQ, including creation, upgrades, and graceful shutdowns. This operator is ideal for users looking to automate RabbitMQ deployments in Kubernetes environments.

steps.title: Add the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repo
steps.description:
This step adds the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add rabbitmq https://rabbitmq.github.io/cluster-operator

```

> This command adds the official RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repository.

steps.title: Install the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator using Helm, specifying the version to ensure compatibility.

```bash

helm install rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --version 2.20.1 --namespace rabbitmq --create-namespace

```

> This command installs the operator in the 'rabbitmq' namespace.

steps.title: Deploy a RabbitMQ cluster
steps.description:
This step deploys a sample RabbitMQ cluster using a predefined YAML configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rabbitmq/cluster-operator/main/docs/examples/hello-world/rabbitmq.yaml

```

> This command applies the RabbitMQ cluster configuration.

steps.title: Verify the RabbitMQ cluster deployment
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the RabbitMQ pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq

```

> This command lists all pods in the 'rabbitmq' namespace to verify their status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator and its associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if persistent volumes are not handled properly.

```bash

helm uninstall rabbitmq-operator --namespace rabbitmq

kubectl delete namespace rabbitmq

```

> This command uninstalls the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator.
> This command deletes the namespace, which may result in loss of all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your RabbitMQ data. This step will upgrade the operator and verify the deployment afterward.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --version 2.20.1 --namespace rabbitmq

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq

```

> This command drains the pods to prepare for the upgrade.
> This command upgrades the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator.
> This command verifies that the pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create RabbitMQ cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error may occur due to insufficient resources or misconfiguration in the YAML file.

**Fix:**
Check the resource limits in your RabbitMQ cluster YAML and ensure your cluster has enough resources.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubectl apply failed: the server could not find the requested resource'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the CRDs are not installed or are outdated.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the CRDs for RabbitMQ are installed by running 'kubectl apply -f https://github.com/rabbitmq/cluster-operator/releases/latest/download/cluster-operator.yml'.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates that the RabbitMQ pods are failing to start due to configuration issues.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n rabbitmq' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'RabbitMQ cluster not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if the RabbitMQ nodes are not able to communicate with each other.

**Fix:**
Verify network policies and service configurations to ensure proper communication between RabbitMQ nodes.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for RabbitMQ 4.1.3, improved health checks, and enhanced logging capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the RabbitMQ pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster and the necessary tools installed.
